questions.q: In Hindu tradition, over what realm does the deity Yama preside?
questions.explanation: Yama is revered as the king of the dead, presiding over departed souls and the afterlife.

questions.q: Which solar deity is named in the tradition as the father of Yama?
questions.explanation: Yama is the son of Surya, the radiant sun god of the Vedas.

questions.q: What animal is described as the vahana (mount) of Yama, lord of death?
questions.explanation: Yama is traditionally depicted mounted upon a buffalo.

questions.q: Which weapon is most associated with Yama for binding and drawing forth departed souls?
questions.explanation: Yama wields a noose (pasha) with which he is said to draw forth the life of the departed.

questions.q: By what title, meaning 'lord of righteousness,' is Yama also honored?
questions.explanation: Yama is also called Dharmaraja, the lord of righteousness, reflecting his role as impartial judge.

questions.q: Which cardinal direction does Yama preside over as a guardian of the quarters?
questions.explanation: Yama presides over the southern direction among the guardian deities of the quarters.

questions.q: Who serves as the scribe of Yama, recording the deeds of every being?
questions.explanation: Chitragupta is the scribe of Yama who records the deeds of departed souls.

questions.q: In addition to a noose, which weapon does Yama, lord of death, carry?
questions.explanation: Along with his noose, Yama is depicted bearing a mace as one of his attributes.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which realm of measured reckoning lies under the rule of Yama?
questions.explanation: Naraka, the realm where souls undergo measured reckoning for their deeds, lies under Yama's rule.

questions.q: What distinction does the tradition give Yama as the very first being to undergo death?
options: The first king of the gods
questions.explanation: Yama is honored as the first mortal to die, who thereby became king over the realm of the dead.

questions.q: In the Vedas, who is named as the twin sister of Yama, sharing his lineage?
questions.explanation: Yami is the twin sister of Yama, with whom he shares his Vedic parentage.

questions.q: In the Katha Upanishad, which young seeker engages Yama in a dialogue on the nature of death and the soul?
questions.explanation: In the Katha Upanishad, the boy Nachiketa receives teachings on death and the eternal soul directly from Yama.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the eldest Pandava Yudhishthira is regarded as the son of which deity?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira, renowned for his righteousness, is regarded as the son of Yama in his aspect as Dharma.

questions.q: In the well-known tale of Savitri, whose life does she follow Yama to recover through her devotion and wisdom?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata's tale, Savitri follows Yama and, through her wisdom, wins back the life of her husband Satyavan.

questions.q: Which name, meaning 'the ender' or 'time,' is also applied to Yama as the bringer of death?
questions.explanation: Yama is also called Kala, meaning time or the ender, as the one who brings all beings to their appointed end.

questions.q: By what name is the city or abode of Yama, where the dead are judged, traditionally known?
questions.explanation: The abode of Yama, where departed souls are brought for judgment, is known as Yamapuri.

questions.q: Which festival celebrates the bond between brother and sister, traditionally linked to Yama and his sister Yami (Yamuna)?
questions.explanation: Bhai Dooj, also called Yama Dvitiya, is traditionally linked to the affectionate meeting of Yama and his sister Yami.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which sage devoted to Shiva is famously rescued from Yama's noose when Shiva intervenes to conquer death?
questions.explanation: The sage Markandeya, clinging to the Shiva linga, is saved when Shiva bursts forth and overcomes Yama's noose.

questions.q: The river Yamuna is traditionally regarded in the Puranas as the sister of which deity?
questions.explanation: The river-goddess Yamuna is traditionally regarded as the sister of Yama, both children of Surya.

questions.q: Which two dreaded dogs are described in the Vedas as the messengers and watchdogs of Yama on the path of the dead?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda describes the four-eyed Sarameya hounds, Shyama and Shabala, as the watchdogs and messengers of Yama.

questions.q: As one of the Lokapalas (guardians of the directions), Yama is grouped with which other deity who guards the west?
questions.explanation: Among the Lokapalas, Yama guards the south while Varuna guards the western direction.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda of the Ramayana, Ravana is said to have marched against and challenged which lord of death during his conquests?
questions.explanation: The Uttara Kanda recounts that the mighty Ravana once contended even with Yama, the lord of death, during his campaigns of conquest.

questions.q: According to the Vedic accounts, Saranyu, the mother of Yama, is the daughter of which divine architect?
questions.explanation: Saranyu, mother of Yama, is named as the daughter of Tvashtar, the divine craftsman of the Vedas.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, Yama is said to have discovered the path for mortals to follow because of which act?
options: His churning of the cosmic ocean
His being the first to traverse the path of death
questions.explanation: The Rigveda honors Yama as the one who first journeyed along the path of death, thereby finding the way for all who follow.

questions.q: Which terrible servant, often identified as Yama's own agent, is the personification dispatched to seize the dying?
questions.explanation: The Yamadutas are the messengers of Yama, dispatched to carry the souls of the departed before him.

questions.q: In the Katha Upanishad, what three boons does Yama grant to the boy who waits at his door?
options: Peace for his father, knowledge of the fire-sacrifice, and the secret of death
A kingdom, an army, and long life
questions.explanation: Yama grants Nachiketa three boons: his father's peace, the knowledge of the sacred fire, and the supreme teaching on death and the Self.

questions.q: In Puranic genealogy, the Manu who presides over the present age, Vaivasvata Manu, shares which parent with Yama?
questions.explanation: Vaivasvata Manu and Yama are both counted among the children of Surya, making them brothers in Puranic lineage.

questions.q: Which staff or rod, symbolizing his authority over the dead, is named as a distinctive emblem of Yama?
questions.explanation: The Yamadanda, the rod or staff of Yama, symbolizes his sovereign authority and the inescapable punishment of wrongdoing.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Yama as Dharma tests Yudhishthira at a lake by appearing in what form before posing his questions?
questions.explanation: In the Yaksha Prashna episode, Yama in the form of a yaksha (crane) questions Yudhishthira before reviving his brothers.

questions.q: The Puranas teach that the suffering souls undergo in Yama's realm of Naraka is best described as which of the following?
options: Measured and proportionate to one's karma before rebirth
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Naraka under Yama not as eternal damnation but as measured suffering, proportionate to one's karma before rebirth.
